linapro-auth-ldap.description: Official source plugin that adds directory (LDAP/AD) sign-in via a login-page form. Depends on External Identity Core.
linapro-auth-ldap.name: LDAP Login

settings.title: LDAP Settings
settings.description: Configure a single LDAP/Active Directory connection. Prefer LDAPS or StartTLS. Use a stable subject attribute (entryUUID / objectGUID).
settings.displayNameHelp: The name shown on the login entry so users can recognize it, for example “Company directory login”.
settings.hostHelp: The directory server address. Use a hostname or IP, for example ldap.example.com.
settings.portHelp: Connection port. Common values: 636 for LDAPS, 389 for StartTLS or plain connections.
settings.tlsModeHelp: How the connection is encrypted. Prefer LDAPS or StartTLS in production. Plain is only for localhost debugging; non-local plain hosts are rejected.
settings.bindDnHelp: The full path of a service account used to look up users in the directory. Leave empty if users bind with their own accounts.
settings.bindPasswordHelp: Password for the service account above. Leave empty to keep the stored password unchanged.
settings.bindPasswordKeepPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep stored password
settings.baseDnHelp: Where in the directory to start looking for users, for example ou=people,dc=example,dc=com. A narrower base is faster and safer.
settings.userFilterHelp: How to find a user by login name. {username} is replaced with what the user typed, for example (uid={username}) or (sAMAccountName={username}).
settings.userDnTemplateHelp: If user paths follow a fixed pattern, build the user location directly, for example uid={username},ou=people,dc=example,dc=com. When set, search by filter can be skipped.
settings.subjectAttrHelp: The attribute used as the stable unique identity for a directory user. Prefer values that do not change, such as entryUUID or objectGUID, not renameable fields.
settings.emailAttrHelp: Directory attribute used for email. Common value: mail.
settings.displayNameAttrHelp: Directory attribute used for the display name. Common values: cn or displayName.
settings.autoProvisionHelp: When on: a first-time directory login that is not linked to a local account creates a least-privilege platform user. When off: unlinked directory accounts cannot sign in.
settings.autoProvisionHint: Off by default. When enabled, verified directory identities without a linked account get a least-privilege platform user.
